reviews.review_text:
After playing all 3 games and 100%  all of them and going back to reply the games twice. I truly have to say Mass effect is one of the greatest trilogy game series I have played.

 ME1 was a great introduction to it all, sure the game is almost 20 years old and has aged in some areas but it has held that iconic role-playing style for its age the gearing in ME1 was fun, enjoyable and oddly addictive. With the people you meet in ME1 and how the relationships and everything carry over to the next 3 games is a nice touch it really makes you feel like you are inside that universe of Mass effect. The story for ME1 really sets the stage for what's to come the more you dive into this series. 

ME2 - the real meal out of the 3 it just enhances everything from the first one more squad-mates, more dialogue between squad-mates, more areas to go to, more of just everything and you can never go wrong with that. The  ONLY niche thing that I wasn't a true fan of in this one was *Spoilers* [spoiler]Such as Liara not being a part of your squad throughout a majority of the game which was pure depression BUT she does have a decent DLC which you do play with her for a few'ish missions[/spoiler] But other than that the game was a enjoyable time its the game that makes you really connect with your squad-mates the most and deep dive into their lore compared to the 1st one so you can carry those feelings and knowledge into the 3rd one. 

ME3 - Now at the beginning I was not a huge fan of how things were going *Spoilers* [spoiler]The reason being is because after Finishing ME2 and doing the suicide mission (which is the ending of ME2) and SAVING everyone I thought this game was gonna be the grand final of it all its gonna have the biggest squad line-up to choose from with new people AND the people I saved, also I thought it was going to have the most people to talk to on the Normandy and boy was I wrong[/spoiler] my biggest gripe with this game was the lack of interaction with your current and previous squad-mates from past games between missions it doesn't have that appeal compared to its last 2 games. BUT other than that the game is amazing it has the best gunplay/class-play out of the 3, the movement feels great its all there  besides that one thing and that's the lack of interaction/engagement that I was so use to in the previous titles. Its still there don't get me wrong but not as strong as it once was. The story for ME3 was a great ride as well now *Spoilers*  [spoiler]The ending on the other hand im 50/50 I picked the paragon ending but I just wish it ended with more of savior theme in a sense  because all 3 endings just end the same route UNLESS you get 8000 war assets and do some other extra stuff then you get a secret cutscene of Shepard breathing for a fraction of a second hinting he/she is alive still after the explosion[/spoiler]. But over all ME3 was a fun time and had the best DLC in any game I've played the Citadel DLC is a must to play-through because its just pure comedy. 

Overall Mass Effect 1,2 and 3 was by far the greatest times I've had in a series. I bought this for 5 bucks when it was on sale one time and let me tell you this is worth full price, getting it for 5 dollars seems like robbery now. I didn't want to get  TO in-depth with describing the games out of the spoiler section and risk spoiling anything  but trust me these 3 games are worth it and they're games where you just have to play them and enjoy the ride. 

- Shepard Out 

-- Liara is best girl --
The Reaper fleet is still out there! Hundreds of ships, maybe thousands, and I am going to find a way to stop them!

I played all three of these in a row and reviewed them individually on Backloggd, so here's my thoughts on all three!  Overall, this release is totally worth your time, ESPECIALLY if you can get it for $5 like I did.  

MASS EFFECT
I tried playing this game back in 2012 and after absolutely HATED it.  Imagine my surprise when I finally give it another go and I fall in love with it.  I love practically everything about this game: the combat, the side quests, the worldbuilding...  Everything!  The game looks a little rough visually sometimes, especially when it comes to character models.  My Shepard is ugly as hell.  But other than that?  Nothing other than small nitpicks.  I wanna jump into the sequel right away!

MASS EFFECT 2
It's odd how much newer, yet different this one feels compared to the first game.  This one feels a lot less tactical than the first one did, but the combat itself did feel more fun.  I do miss the Mako from the first game, this game's replacement is a lot less fun, despite the platforming changes it makes.  The different mission structure this time around was interesting, too.  In a way, it almost felt like there weren't main missions to do until the very end, when I ended up completing everything.  The new characters were cool, and it was great seeing some decisions I made in the previous game pay off in one way or another.  Overall, I still think this game is incredible, but I think the first one was a bit better.

MASS EFFECT 3
It's really interesting just how linear these games ended up becoming by the end.  While it was neat that just about every mission felt substantial in one way or another, I did kind of miss that sense of exploration from some of the earlier games.  But because of the narrative here, I can see why the devs wouldn't want you to explore as much this time around.  But I did miss it.

I think the game is pretty solid overall, but less enjoyable than the other two entries.  And if I'm being honest, I think the DLCs kind of brings the experience down.  The Citadel DLC was especially egregious, I really did not enjoy that one at all.  It doesn't really make sense in the overarching story, and the tone is waaaaay different than the rest of the game.  

I did particularly like some of the quality of life improvements; the vaulting button shortcuts and other environment traversal movement additions were pretty welcome here.  The War Assets feature was a pretty interesting addition that did a good job at simultaneously showing progress, and the new visual style was mostly nice to look at.  I think it's a bit jarring in some instances, but I ultimately think the game looks pretty solid.

Overall, this game was a pretty fitting end to the series.  It's not perfect, but when looking at the main parts of the story, I think everything works for the most part.  Very happy I gave this series another chance after not liking the Xbox release almost 15 years ago.
I love the series. This edition is more often than not unusable on steam deck purely because of the EA launcher. dont buy it until they actually fix it for deck.

Easily one of the best story games I’ve ever played. The story is incredibly well written, the worlds are nicely designed, and the choices you make throughout the game can REALLY affect how events unfold. I think the combat system could use some improvement, especially when moving between covers, especially when playing as a solider, but it has definitely improved across the games.
Great QoL update and tons of new mods out for the game that round off some major edges. Worst part of the game is that it launches the EA software/EA launcher in the background.

Update Jun 3rd 2026: I checked the game out again after a recent small patch, and this time it runs as it previously did, perfectly fine. Still the point stands that an external launcher with online-only DRM shouldn't be required for a single-player title. Just be aware you can purchase this game and loose access to it at any time.

-----

EA has done an EA. By that I mean for this single-player game they've lumped in their awful launcher, and on top of that they've recently updated it which completely breaks compatibility with steam deck / Linux. This game should NOT be marked as 'playable' on steam deck as it simply won't launch. Even using proton experimental, or proton 11, or deleting the prefix - the EA app presents the message "An error on our end prevented the game launching, try again later". 

It should not be acceptable this day and age for 'an error on our end' from EA, to prevent a game I purchased from working.

Words can’t describe how amazing this game is.

My advice to new players: Be patient and stick with it in ME1, you will find plenty of surprises along the way.
This is one of the best game series of all time, and the this remaster makes it so much better! I can't help but replay it over and over again.

Whether you’re already a die-hard fan or have never even heard of the series, you seriously need to play this. These games are just so much fun. The storytelling keeps you engaged, the characters are interesting, and the combat is engaging. I cannot recommend it enough—just grab it and start playing.

Back when the name Bioware used to mean something we got this awesome trilogy  of space-opera rpg games that are unsurpassed even today. 
I first played this on Xbox 360 at my local Internet café when  was just a kid and it stayed with me through all these years. Now I have replayed it with the same Shepard  I did back then to relieve one of the greatest stories ever told in gaming.
Today's Bioware could never make something like this and it is clear why they have fallen, their games from almost 2 decades ago are better and more impactful then anything they put out recently.

Played the trilogy for the first time and absolutely loved it. Everyone knows the Mass Effect games are classics, but you have to experience them yourself to really understand why.
The EA app can cause a few annoying issues but I still think this version is worth getting especially since it lets you experience everything in one place.
(Note: If you want to 100% it, just start playing on Insanity from the beginning, you’ll get used to the gameplay in no time.)

This is a massive sci-fi epic that spans 3 games. I loved exploring the galaxy and its many species, each with their own history, politics, and culture. With the Legendary Edition, you can easily import your character to the next games with all your choices dynamically affecting the narrative. The trilogy has so many incredible characters, each with their own great moments. On top of that, the music is sublime and perfectly fits the sci-fi theme. A truly galactic-scaled story, anyone who is a fan of RPGs or sci-fi absolutely needs to play Mass Effect.

This is an incredibly great game: if you want to save the universe by leading a crew of friends on a cinematic adventure, this is it. Great voice acting. Good graphics. Good gameplay. Lots of choices that can affect the story. Loved it and I can't wait to play more Mass Effect games!

Possibly the greatest trillogy ever made.  Story is amazing.  Gameplay gets better with each iteration.

Mass effect 1 can feel like a slog when compared to 2 and 3 but the world building is so good its worth it.

Never had a game come close the achieving what this trillogy achieved.

I couldn't recommend this game more!
Great games, but unfortunately F EA and F their launcher. Respect your customers if you want better reviews.

Overall it is a quite good game trilogy. And this remaster did it justice. Unlike other remasters which may just increase texture resolution without much else (causing things to look worse), this game doesn't do that. In terms of quality it's up there with black mesa for me. I only had one time where the game crashed and thankfully autosave made it so I barely lost any progress.

ME1:
By far the game I enjoyed in terms of gameplay the most. There wasn't as much emphasis on cover shooting, I preferred the non-magazine based gunplay. The level design felt more open compared to the next games, less linear, because even if there were hallways often you had at least some choice in multi-step missions as to which part you wanted to do first. The morality system in the game was my least favorite as it involves you needing to place upgrades in an upgrade tree instead of being purely based on choices. Although, the skill tree itself I preferred compared to ME2/3. It also had my favorite hacking minigames. In terms of writing however, individual characters felt a little bit more stale compared to the next two games. Although, when it comes to hard choices, I think the game did better than ME2/3 (there are non-negotiable character deaths but you can choose who lives and who dies). 

ME2:
In general, gameplay felt much more linear. The addition of ammo/clips and relying more on cover shooting wasn't something I was a fan of (Also the lore justification for it is silly). Ammo was so plentiful that it didn't feel like it mattered very much playing through on a normal difficulty. Which also as a result meant that you didn't have to worry about not spamming a weapon too much like in the first game. As a result, sniper rifles felt way more powerful which is sort of funny. The upgrade tree of the game felt worse and less interesting than the first game, but I prefer the new paragon/renegade system as it was only exclusive to decisions you made. I liked the hacking minigames though not as much as the first game. I thought the resource/scanning minigames were fun though. The game focuses way more on characters and story and in a good way for the most part compared to the previous game. The cast of characters is a little bit TOO large if anything. I think this games biggest mistake with the story is allowing you to be able to save everyone though. Unlike the first game, if you do everything right you can manage to get away without much consequences. Additionally, the introduction of Cerberus is a bit railroaded for anyone who was paying attention to their lore in the first game. 

ME3:
By far I think it had to worst gameplay of the series. It really really forced cover shooting gameplay way more than the other two games. I basically just picked the Protheon assault gun and stacked it with my squad mates so I was able to mildly recreate the gameplay I enjoyed from the first game at least. There are a few sections in the gameplay where there are tracking oneshot attacks that have sort of jank ways to avoid them which are a little bit annoying. The upgrade tree requiring you to go into an embedded UI every time is not user friendly. The lack of minigames probably had to do with them getting out of fashion at the time, but I was kind of sad to see them go. The gap between ME2/3 in the story made a bit more sense between ME1/2 but there were still some issues as to how Shepard actually got 'grounded' which wasn't explained enough. The map of ME3 I think also was a little bit worse than the previous games, you couldn't ping locations like in the previous two games for some reason. The Illusive man's story arc was pretty decent. Kai Leng though is the world's first 0 dimensional character. Dude has the personality of a half-dried brick. I liked the endings and the buildup to it. I am not a fan of how you make the choice for your endings, it is rather confusing as it does not involve a dialogue choice whereas like 99% of these similar important choices are made through dialogue options and it doesn't explain well enough how this choice will not be like that. There is of course, only one 'correct' choice for which ending you take.... yes I'm totally on board with a popular conspiracy theory. 

Overall though great game, and while it has its flaws the story does make up for it. Something I think everyone should try playing at least once.

I am Commander Shepard, and this is my favorite trilogy on Steam.

It took me way too long to finally give these games a chance, and I'm glad I did. Very few games have ever gotten me as invested in every aspect of them as this trilogy did. The story and worldbuilding are phenomenal, but what truly got me invested were the characters and how they evolved and developed a deeper bond with Shepard as the games went on. The ending of the third game might have dropped the ball pretty hard at the very last minutes, but it by no means ruins the rest.

Now that I've finished these games, I feel a bit sad. Sad that I won't see these characters again, these characters who I've come to like so much. Much like it was with Baldur's Gate 3, I feel like I'm saying goodbye to my dearest friends. I can replay these games, but it won't be the same.

Keelah se'lai.
Its old, but it still hits hard, I love it so far, I wish I had played it when it was new. However one can only play so may damn games.
I cannot recommend this version due to the bloatware that is installed along the game. Better get another version or even play the free edition.

(ME 1)
The navigation system is absolutely terrible. 
There's no active, real-time tracking system for objects or quests.
The game essentially feels like a giant sandbox with some objectives littered around. 
All I do is switch back and forth between the journal and the map, hoping to find the right path.

These developers really want players to remember every single name of every corner.
[h2] I did not enjoy a single bit of this game. Just force myself to complete this game. [/h2] 

Then I started Mass Effect 2. 
And wow I can't believe this game is just one step backwards from its previous title.
This game doesn't even have any MAP. 
Yeah !! An open world RPG game with no map at all!! How beautiful !!!
Man ... I really need to stop taking suggestions from Gen X people and stop spending money on these $#!t games.
release the official soundtracks for steam.... Anyways masterpiece I've been addicted to since 2019

I finally finished Mass Effect Legendary Edition after 80+ hours and honestly, this might be one of the best gaming experiences I’ve ever had. I went into it expecting a good sci-fi RPG, but I didn’t expect to get this attached to the characters and story.

The first game starts a little slow and definitely feels older gameplay-wise, but the worldbuilding is insane. By the time I got into Mass Effect 2, I was completely hooked. The squadmates are easily the best part of the trilogy. Characters like Garrus Vakarian, Liara T'Soni, Tali'Zorah, and even EDI actually feel like real people you grow with over three games. I genuinely cared about what happened to them.

What makes the trilogy special is that your choices actually matter. Small decisions from the first game can affect things way later in the story, and seeing those consequences pay off was amazing. Some moments in Mass Effect 3 genuinely hit hard emotionally. The whole war atmosphere, the music, the conversations with your crew — it all feels bigger and more personal than most RPGs I’ve played.

The Legendary Edition itself is also a really good remaster. The graphics look much cleaner, the lighting is better, load times are fast, and Mass Effect 1 especially feels way smoother to play now. Having almost all the DLC included makes it feel complete too.

It’s not perfect though. Some side missions can get repetitive, and parts of the combat in the first game feel clunky compared to modern games. The ending is still controversial depending on what choices you make, but honestly, the journey there was so good that it didn’t ruin the experience for me.

Overall, Mass Effect Legendary Edition is one of those games that stays in your head after you finish it. It’s funny, emotional, intense, and sometimes heartbreaking in the best way possible. If you like story-driven games with great characters and choices that matter, you need to play this trilogy.

10/10 for me.

You need to provide EA your data to play. Cannot play offline. Need internet and don't have actual ownership of the game. The franchise is incredible but me and my fellow gamers are sick of not owning what we buy - especially for a $60 pricetag! Rapacious greed will lose customers. Let me play offline.

If you must get this game, please just get it when it is on sale. If you want to know why I felt a little let down by what was one of the best Sci-Fi settings ever, here is a refresher:

+ Mass Effect 1 Was released in November 2007 by Bioware. (An amazing studio)
+ Electronic Arts acquires Bioware and Pandemic Studios in January 2008
+ Changes to Bioware as a Studio are made... and the next games become like Products instead of works of Art.
+ Every studio wants their Call of Duty (for the massive profit), so the dialogue becomes more "on rails" than anything more engaging. At least, that is how Mass Effect 3 felt for me.

Edit: I completed the game, getting the [spoiler] Destroy Ending with 7800+ Readiness [/spoiler]

[h3] *Major spoilers ahead, so be warned.* [/h3]


In the end, all I felt was that ME3 was truly rushed. I kind of get what they were going for... My perspective is that [spoiler] Shepard's mind is collapsing due to constant exposure to Reaper Technology, Reaper enemies, and weird stuff happening to his/her brain after being exposed to Prothean Beacons/Asari Mind Melds/Geth Consensus (if you keep Legion). To me, even exceptional people like Shep have their limits. [/spoiler] Which is why the end of the last mission seems so much like... [spoiler] Brain Damage Induced Hallucinations, or someone's mind putting together a dream from different bits and pieces of stuff they remember. [/spoiler]

If the developers had more time, and more feedback was given to the writing team so that the ending was less vague, *and* if there was a post ending scene/dialogue like in Baldur's Gate 3, the reception to it would have been so much better. The Director's Cut changes in the Legendary Edition does help a bit, but it still feels like there wasn't enough time to breathe *after* the final mission/cutscene.

I won't list spoilers because this game deserves to be spoiler free for newer gamers.

I played all three of the original games the trilogy covers. While Legendary Edition has some issues, it's still far better than playing the original games.

Performance shouldn't be an issue on newer systems. Most of the games have refresh rate options up to 144Hz, and also support vsync for a tear free experience on older GPUs.

I sunk over 200 hours into the original games, and just sunk another 200 hours into re-playing them.

And I am considering a third playthrough already. That should tell you everything that you need to know about the original Mass Effect series.

Im commander shephard and this is a 10/10 game, love how choices matter for the follow up games and that u will even see side quest npc's that thank and help you, great story lovely characters (tali). GG
Get your ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ spyware launcher app off my system, EA. I do business with the Valve Corporation, not you.

Two out of three ain't bad.

A wise man shared these words of wisdom with us decades ago, and he wasn't wrong. Mass Effect 1 and Mass Effect 2 still stand out as fantastic sci-fi gaming classics. I liked the remaster work they did one ME1. Made the combat much better than when I played it on 360 all those years ago. Never played the DLC for ME2 before, and I have to say I think it messed with the pacing of the game narrative a little bit, but Overlord and LotSB were excellent. ME3...well let's just say hat tricks are celebrated for a reason, and this trilogy in my mind fell short of getting one. A solid game with one of the worst narrative finales I have ever experienced. 

Way more bad than good, and with all three games the amount of content you get is hard argue with. If I had to break it down by game:

ME1 - 9/10
ME2 - 8/10
ME3 - 6/10

Just completely amazing, even with some of the bugs, I would say this game is flawless, emotional and so worthy it from the first to the third game, doing all the quests was a blast and felt engaging enough. I would just say ♥♥♥♥ it with doing the 100% of the planets gathering resources on me2, what a bs and so useless. 10/10.

Mass Effect 1: 9/10 Still holds up, combat feels dated, but is in line with games of the era
Mass Effect 2: 9/10 Suicide mission simulator with romance.
Mass Effect 3: 5/10 An excellent game dragged down by gaming's WORST ending(s).  With a different ending it would have been 9/10. Bioware chose poorly.
This is my favourite game ever. I've always loved the franchise. The gameplay is wonderful, I love the story, I love Garrus even more <3

I love the series and i had over 4k hours playing each game and enjoyed every time i ran through the campaign . First time playing on the PC but I'm glad i get to try it out on here. Also can't wait for the 4th one to come out and going to try andromeda on here as well I just hope they keep the series going a bit longer and not end on the 4th one.

EA forces you to use a launcher that DOES NOT WORK! What a piece of ♥♥♥♥ company. Completely ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ruined the game because the launcher refuses to cooperate. Tried multiple bug fixes. It should be flat out illegal to force customers to use a launcher separate from the platform the game/license was purchased from. Total ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ garbage.
ea is ran by chuds and their stupid ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ launcher is useless garbage

I've just finished mass effect 1. Although the game is fairly old and the gameplay is quite simple, the originality of the story compensates those. It's a 3d adventure that offers you choices as you play. You can be the standard good or a really funny evil character.

The improvements to ME1 save it from most of the esoteric jankiness that has caused the original release to age quite poorly from a gameplay perspective. It still plays inferior to ME2 and certainly to ME3, both of which retain identical gameplay to their original releases. 

The package as a whole is easily at least 80+ hours of gaming that portrays one of the best and most clever sci-fi universes in any medium.  On principle and bias I want to give this a 10. In reality, the subtle clunkiness of the gameplay in ME1 and the slight but noticeable decline of ME3's writing quality make this a strong 9. (I have criticisms about ME2 as well, particularly its overall narrative structure, but they are hard to convey without spoilers, so I will leave it at that).

Without a doubt, the Mass Effect trilogy remains a must-play.

Hard to say anything about Mass Effect that hasn't already been said at this point. One of my favorite gaming trilogies. Having all of the DLC completes the package. Just wish it had ME3 multiplayer.

There are some minor graphical issues with the trilogy on PC, but overall it's still a great buy. I'd recommend the PS4 version over Steam/EA App if you have a choice.

Mass Effect 1 had HDR issues and kept defaulting to extremely high contrast, and Mass Effect 3 had a bug that kept defaulting the resolution to 1280 × 720 if I went into the visuals menu after loading a save file. Mass Effect 2 crashed whenever I had Discord open on a second monitor. May be a false positive, but it only crashed the two times I had Discord open.
Exacting payment as a means of imposing limits upon the Truth is an abrogation of this one's religious freedom

I promise I am being 100% serious when I say this is the single greatest, most compelling and most emotional story of any video game I've ever played. Better than baldur's gate, better than god of war, better than skyrim. The gameplay is somewhat generic for a third-person shooter, but that's to be expected of a nearly 20 year old game. It regularly goes on sale for £5, and one playthrough can easily be over 150 hours. Honestly one of the top 10 games of all time.
Excellent story and characters, fun combat (although sometimes annoying lack of mobility, you cannot jump).

The games and their DLC in one package, the ability to seamlessly play through all 3 with your choices is great. they fixed many bugs from the originals and patched things but there are areas/sequences in some of the games that still have bugs or crashes especially on newer computers that run too fast for these old games so sometimes you have to reduce graphics (especially refresh rate) to stop crashes temporarily. Other than that, lots of fun to replay through these old goodies

This set of games is amazing. Each of the three games brings its own unique style of gameplay. Each game improves upon the last. I always remember Mass Effect 2 having tons of insane cut scenes and kind of boredom... I will say that has been changed with the legendary edition. I just completed all three games with all achievements at 183hrs. I am a very happy camper. I did my first run through on insanity through all three games and managed to complete every single mission in all 3 games with the exception of one mission in Mass Effect three DLC... It was Aria's Couch. I simply didn't do enough exploring to find it. I was very happy to see that I was able to complete everything these games had to offer even running on the highest difficulty. I did get stuck a couple times in Mass Effect three that required me to rage quit for a couple of weeks. One of them was the Suppressor mission in ME3. I had played the original trilogy when it first came out and to replay it now was a blessing years later. 

I highly recommend this series for anyone who loves single player RPG stories. Do steer clear of the fourth installment unless you are looking for lore dumps or just have time to kill. 

Cheers!

Amazing collection. I invested an equal amount of time in this one as I did in the original series for the main story. The only thing that I don't like is that there isn't any online multiplayer. I bought the game on N7 day so I forgave them since I didn't get it at full price, but I would've been upset if I paid full price and didn't get the multiplayer on ME3. I have a separate copy of ME 3 from my original collection that I keep installed just in case I want to play multiplayer.

Honestly my friend introduced me to the game on ps5 and I was like damn I kinda wanna play it for myself. I have a ps5 too but I realize there was mods and had to get it on my laptop. I barely review games but this game truly holds a special place in my heart and the story is a beautiful one that you make yourself. I always say to play the game first time mod free but the second you finish that third game I highly recommend modding the ♥♥♥♥ out of this game makes it 10X more replayable and soooo much fun!!!

Excellent port of some of the best games of all time.

I keep telling my friend Nick to play this trilogy but he won't so if you see Nick tell him to play mass effect already. Nick would love mass effect
Brilliant games, forget achievements etc. They're nerffed. MS 2 is knackred for me too.

The game is good.
EA experience is not worth your time. Get this game by any other means, the EA launcher completely devalues this game, especially on the Deck. 
The Deck plays it fine, controls and graphics are great, but the launcher makes the experience absolutely dreadful every step of the way.

It’s one of the weirdest trilogies I’ve played in terms of consistency.

ME1 is easily the game that benefits the most from the Legendary Edition upgrade. It still feels old in some areas, but the worldbuilding is excellent. The universe, the races, the politics, the mystery around the Reapers, everything feels big and interesting. I also really liked the weapon system because it actually feels like there is some RPG depth there.

Quests can be hard to find, hard to track, and sometimes you are just wandering around trying to understand where you are supposed to go. But even with that, it has a certain charm and identity that the later games don’t fully keep.

ME2 is where things get strange for me. In terms of story, characters, loyalty missions, and side quests, it is great. The squad is probably the best part of the whole trilogy. But gameplay-wise, I think it is a downgrade in many ways.

They removed the proper map, simplified the weapon system, and somehow made weapons more confusing because you don’t really get clear stats. The combat also becomes very repetitive: enter arena, hide behind cover, shoot waves of enemies, repeat. There are just so many enemies, and after a while it starts feeling grindy. I still enjoyed the story a lot, but the actual playing part became tiring.

ME3 looks better and definitely has the best movement and shooting. The combat feels smoother, faster, and more polished. But after spending around 80 hours in ME2, I was already tired, so maybe that affected my experience.

Still, ME3 felt like it had lost a lot of the character of the first game. It starts feeling more like an arcade shooter than an RPG. The maps feel very closed, with obvious predetermined paths, enemies keep spawning, and there is still no proper map, just general directions. At some point I ditched it after a few hours and watched the rest of the story on YouTube. I just wanted to fight the Reapers, instead I’m doing fetch quests around the galaxy because the plot says so. I just couldn’t care anymore.


The universe is at stake and you still solve small bickering between nations, or in order for them to help you, you need to do something for them, and it usually is they send you for a fetch quests like a dog. Like dude, it's your universe too, tf???

Even the assassin working for Cerberus is a comic villain, he is smart enough to know what is at stake and still behaves the way he does just because.

Then there is the ending. After all these hours, all these choices, all this buildup, the explanation and the final outcome felt cheap. What you did during all your game none really matters. None of the endings felt satisfying to me, they were slapped together to conclude this epic adventure story.

For a trilogy that spends so much time making you believe your decisions matter, in the end is all complete ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥.

Overall i think it's a lazy upgrade, they did not do any quality of life improvements and all 3 games suffer heavily from problems the games of that era had, but we have solved today. 

No need to invest so much hours to be honest, just watch the story video on the internet. The finale will leave you with a bad taste in your mouth.
This game, 20 years old from the orginal, still beats most titles. incredibly addictive. stay away if you have a family

Very disappointed, i wanted to get into the series for the first time, but whenever i launched the first game it would lag severly causing it to run at like 2 fps and for the audio to desync. so, being unable to start the game properly i forgot about it for some time until i decided to try running it again to no luck. My pc is not at fault for this, it has no problem running much beefier game that weren't made in the 2000s. Seeing no other solution, i tried refunding the collection only to be refused because too much time had passed after i bought it, i think by literally a few hours or a day. Thankfully, i saw that it is on game pass, which means i'll be giving it a go again. tl;dr if the game has any performance problems, fix them or refund the game, dont wait for it to fix itself like i did. i want my money back.

I remember having played Mass Effect 1 years ago when I was a kid, didn't remember much of the plot but I remembered having a good time with it. I even have the CD stashed in the attic somewhere.

I recently decided to buy the trilogy and give it a shot after having it be recommended to me by a friend, and I don't regret it in the slightest - hands down one of the best games I ever played. The story is rich and captivating, the world really pulls you in, and you quickly fall in love with the characters you recruit, the attachment only grows as you progress through different missions together. My unquestionable favorite being of course none other than Garrus.

I'm now considering buying Mass Effect: Andromeda despite the negative opinions I heard about it in the past, just to be able to return to the in-game universe again.

BioWare is working on another Mass Effect project that would return to the story from the original trilogy.
I wish them a great success, and hope that they took enough time for necessary calibrations.
Probably the most unique gaming experience around. Choices that carry through all 3 games are few and far between. This will always be a yearly playthrough for me

I got so invested into the story of this game. I LOVE IT SO MUCH. Such good world building in each game. The characters or funny and amazing to interact with. You build a connection to the characters as your actions carry on throughout the 3 games. This game holds the #1 spot for best games of all time. I tell everyone to give this game a try as it is just a masterpiece (In my opinion ofc). Will put more hours into 100%ing the game.

Wish it was remade the way Bethesda did Oblivion, rather than making the ALOT mods official, but it's still my beloved Mass Effect with some extra juice. I can't possibly dislike something from Bioware's golden era of writing quality.

What can I say, for me this game trilogy is it, the GOAT. First played through it when the series launched on xbox 360 and loved it then. And now this is the best way to play it. Everything about this trilogy is what makes gaming great: a fantastic setting, tremendous stories in the large and in the small, memorable characters, memorable missions and great gameplay. And what this trilogy did with game continuity through save file transfers needs to be studied and used more as it pushes the emotional investment into the outcome through the roof as your decisions have real consequences. Sure, it is not perfect and these days in particular part 1 can feel a little dated, but how this trilogy creates a story that builds and builds and gets you invested is beyond equal. I refused to embark on the final assault mission for a week on my first playthrough just because I did not want it to end, something no other game has ever achieved with me. Ever since I have always bought this game for any platform I game on. There is nothing else to say, you must play this game.

If you like Sci-Fi and you like RPG's you honestly owe it to yourself to play the Mass Effect trilogy. It's peerless and makes you invested in both the setting and characters in such a way that im utterly convinced this is Bioware's best work. I find it hard to believe I'll play a Sci-Fi RPG as good as this for the rest of my life.

In my view this is one of the best video game trilogies and series of all time. Yes, the third game's ending is flawed, but passable with the extended cut. I did wish how some of the story's direction went in a different discussion, but that has been discussed to death by now. 

For this collection you get all three games. They revamped the first game's combat a little bit with some updated UI, and a little easier to use your abilities. But it still janky since they basically remastered the game for modern hardware. The inventory system is still a massive list that is annoying to sort through, and many side missions reuse assets for the bunkers. That said, overall the game is still a blast and has held up well in most areas.

The visuals, while keeping the original art style in tact, did a good job revamping things with the series. You see new lighting in places like Noveria, better draw distance, and overall texture improvements with the first game. Mass Effect 2 and 3 has some improvements, but not as notable as the first game. To me visually both games held up well with the original editions, but they made some minor changes like improved lighting to reflections in 2 and 3 as well. Overall the games run well too.

If you can bypass the dumb ♥♥♥♥ EA login, then this is a great experience. 
If you have to get on your knees and lick EA's harries, then it's far less of a pleasant experience. Go through the trouble of removing the log in.

The Mass Effect games really bring you back to a time when developers cared about the quality of their games.
A most legendary game. Come back and finish the trilogy every year for the past half decade.

I am only playing these  games because these are my brother's favorite games of all time. As a kid I would watch him quietly play the game on our shared computer. I didn't really understand anything. Sometimes I was allowed to choose the armour of some characters. Now as an adult, I get to experience what he enjoyed so much. Even though I am not a fan of shooter-games, I must say that I really like the world and the characters. The games can be ridiculous at times, but that's okay. Pretty good stuff!

I recommend this game but i give it 3/5 overall.
Parts 1&2 are nice to play, part 3 is really bad,corny and cheesy dialogues,Hero crying and feeling sad,no thats not ok.I spend 100 hours in parts 1-2 and i do not even finish part 3.

Also as a big BIOWARE fan (i play their games or their engine games since BG,Icewind Dale,Neverwinter,DA series,KOTOR) i find plot and story very basic,every companion has a sad story side quest and i feel 
like i am in the middle of a large therapy group.
I blame EA for this .

Buy it cheap if you find it but i think is an overhyped franchise with shallow story and boring combat system,just hide-shoot-hide-shoot.

I played. I laughed. I cried. I c-u-m. 
One of the best journeys you will ever go on. A band of characters that are unforgettable. A game that will make you leak bodily fluid, one way or another. [spoiler](Miranda's ass) [/spoiler]

Easily the best videogame trilogy of all time. I just played it again with all games played back-to-back. In contrast to playing it when the series first came out you really feel the weight of your decisions as if they just happened (instead of two years ago). Do yourself a favor, play this series. The graphics, gameplay, story, choices, etc. are all phenomenal (graphics-wise the third is obviously best).
Just overall amazing game play, great story arcs, and wonderful loyalty missions.

One of the best sci-fi series I've ever experienced and/or played. So many possible builds, playstyles, interactions, etc.; it's just massive. The "perfect" ending for ME3 better be the one leading into ME4 - if it ever gets made.

great game
♥♥♥♥ company
Add the Mp back you parasites it was p2p hosting.

Just finished a full playthrough of Legendary Edition on Insanity as a Sentinel, this may be one of the greatest games ever made the story is sublime going through each game having lasting consequences for squad members npc's & events is something you do not see in other games. (I know on release the endings got significant backlash) but honestly after viewing them all last night.

My favourite is The Synthesis, but I have left my final save on Destroy (Perfect) ready for the hope of Mass Effect 4.

The game evolves from Mass Effect 1 > Mass Effect 2 then to Mass Effect 3, you can see the streamlining of systems and combat from one to the other, as much as I enjoyed the Mako in the first game I was much happier scanning planets & even happier in the final instalment just launching a probe on specific planets. 

The gun combat going from the first game just managing heat to the final game managing ammo was a positive change in my opinion. (I used power combos in no.3 and did not really use a pistol unless I had to until I got the M-11).

My only negative was the fact you had to mess around on Mass Effect 2 to keep the Liara romance so you could get the Paramour (1, 2 & 3) Achievement ofc she was not really in the game until Shadow Broker, so it is understandable.

Mass Effect 1 Squad - Sentinel Commander Shepard (Male), Garrus Vakarian & Urdnot Wrex.
Mass Effect 2 Squad - Sentinel Commander Shepard (Male), Garrus Vakarian & Miranda Lawson.
Mass Effect 3 Squad - Sentinel Commander Shepard (Male), Garrus Vakarian(Ascended Garrus) & Liara T'Soni (Biotic Bomb).

Hope this helps others play this great game as it is definitely a must play for anyone who enjoys action, role playing or just wants a good story.

I'm Commander Shepard, and this is my favourite game.

Great trilogy. 
Mass Effect 1 sets the beginning of the story so good and the RPG mechanics are shown best in this game. A good classic.
Mass Effect 2 is just one giant filler in between two bangers, but it's still alright, although the level-up system went down the drain in this one. The character development for most squadmates really kicks off here and is excellent. 
Mass Effect 3 is a really good finale game, though a lot of people don't like the ending, but from what I remember, the added stuff they have in for the end makes it more bearable. The combat is at its peak in this game, though the RPG mechanics are still not as good as the first game. Character development for squadmates finalizes in this game excellently, and most of the side plots such as the genophage and the geth-quarian conflict is finalized in some manner in this game as well (of course, how those plot lines end depends on your choices). Also, you get to see how much of a goat Admiral Hackett is in this game.
For those who want to know what your ending war asset score should be by the end of ME3, I believe you need to aim for 7400, though I have comments on in case someone wishes to correct that.

A very faithful update for ME1, ME2, and ME3. Lack of dialog cutscene 21:9 ratio and in-engine cutscenes is frustrating, and there are some crashing and audio bugs, but overall a good package. Nice visual updates and a couple quality of life improvements. Don't get bogged down with ME1's side missions and probing all of the planets in ME2 - that'll burn you out real quick.

Fun to play these games again with the DLCs incorporated. Never played Bringing Down the Sky, Project Overlord, Leviathan, and so on. Definitely a good bit of game at a low price.

As someone who's been playing Mass Effect since launch this is the best way to revisit the games today.

The world building is some of the very best in all of gaming. The games aren't perfect, but they are so fun, interesting and repayable. Filled with such a memorable cast of characters that you genuinely get attached to. 

Playing through them all back to back is a must and took me around 100 hours to do and that's with doing nearly all optional side-content. As the games go on they're increasingly filled with self-aware and so many nods to the fans and the previous games that it's impossible not to smile. Whether you've played these games before or this is your first time, it is worth playing through the trilogy in this package with all the DLC's. It truly feels like how the games were always meant to be experienced.

So far, I've only played Mass Effect 1 and 2. But both games are pretty solid. The story line is good. The game mechanics are good. No real complaints. If you catch this on sale, it's an excellent deal.

I can't even run this because it keeps crashing the second I try to start a new game in Mass Effect 1. I don't recommend the legendary addition. Just get the individual games unless you wanna risk wasting $60 for nothing.

I'm Commander Shepard and this is my favorite game on the Citadel!

What can I say that hasn't already been said about these games? Each one was a masterpiece when it released and this collection/remaster is as well. Mass Effect 1 received the heaviest remaster treatment, but in all honesty, the graphics of the originals hold up relatively well. This is about all I could ever ask for or want out of a remaster; just a little touch up here and there, but leaving the experience mostly as it was.

There's not much to say besides "You should play it", because if I could Men In Black erase my memory to experience any one game/series again, Mass Effect would take that honor over anything else. Every few years I get the itch to replay it, and simply typing this review right now is reigniting the flame.

This is my favorite game ever I think. The fact that the legendary edition was just a collection with slightly updated textures and lighting is somewhat disappointing. I would love to see these games with updated animations and some tweaks to make story decisions more impactful, but overall I love these games and getting them all for $60 (or $5 if you get it on sale which happens all the time) is such a good deal. Would absolutely recommend giving it a whole playthrough and experimenting for yourself the ways you can effect the world and your crew.

i know EA, BIoware, hell, just about every AAA game dev out there is just waiting to suck the marrow out of anything we enjoy right now. But this is, in my honest opinion, not that. This is a time capsule. To a story that shook every si-fi shooter, every story buff, everyone who loved to just play a game. It's something important to everyone that loves this series. Sure it's not perfect (we are missing a dlc from the first game) but despite that it's a good memory to a time when we all loved Bioware's creations. And perhaps  someday, maybe we'll love them again.

I only heard the bits and bobbles of the Mass Effect games, mostly regarding three and it's "ending" debacle. I also got this game on sale idk how long ago and just never played it until recently and after sitting down, playing the trilogy, and then having it sit awhile. I have come to a conclusion.

The trilogy is good... It has it's flaws of course, and I don't think anyone will ever forgive or forget the writers who went into a room for three days, wrote that "ending" for ME3, and refused to cooperate with anyone else on the writing team. But it is a 95% amazing game, with 4% being weird or wonky stuff, and the 1% being the "pick a color" that sours the experience at the end of the day.

One thing I will say is that, the games have the weird vibe that I can only put in an equally weird way. "Saren? Collectors? Reapers? Who cares about those? I wanna instead go on goofy space adventures with my crew of borderline war criminals and psychopaths." The games make you care so much about the smaller things like crew interactions between each other and their own personal struggles, that it ironically makes you care less about the bigger things aka the main story plot lines. And it is hard to say that if that is a good thing or a bad thing, so I am just gonna put in as a "trade-off" that doesn't add or subtract from the overall experience.

One of if not the best trilogies ever made, the amount of love depth into actions that travel across games. The love for the characters old and new, minor and major. Gameplay for the most part evolves with each game feeling like actual growth and learning from player feedback in the old days. iv sunk more hours into this "old" game trilogy than any current triple a game in the past 5 years. Give it a try or relive old memories

So before I get to the game it is important that you know that to play the game you need to install EA's spyware app. That will constantly run in the background while you are playing their games, scanning your PC for as many details as it can find. Doesnt hurt performance but scummy work EA

Anyway, so legendary edition is all 3 of the original mass effect games (a thousand times better then the new ones) and all of their DLC, with some improvements. Collectively you will beat all of them within 90-130 hours individually they are quite short. There’s enough replayability for 2 play throughs no real NG+

Story: Mass effect is a game with a story that builds up over time. The first game’s forgettable story exists just to ease you into the franchise. It only gets better from there. Some tips, make sure you do every loyalty mission in the second game as soon possible or there will be consequences. And in the third game the ending is dependent on how many war assets you gather. Don’t get enough and you get the bad endings. MS3 gets rushed at the end that’s because they hit deadline and had to cut content like the final boss fight but I loved the game at least after all the lost launch content they put in to fix the story.

The gameplay is quite easy so play on hardcore or higher, your enjoyment of gameplay with be dependent on your class and besides the first game every class has very unique gameplay. Recommend looking up their details on the wiki
good game, good story, good characters. play this ♥♥♥♥.

Absolute cinema.

I did not play these games back when they came out a decade ago, so I did not know what expect from them. But I was pleasantly surprised. These games focus much more on narrative and dialogue exploration than combat. Combat is your 2010s cover shooter type deal and it stays pretty consistent between all the 3 games. 

There's a lot of content and the cool thing about the trilogy is that you can port your character over from the previous games and there are choices in the earlier games that can have an effect on the sequels. 

Grabbed the game on 90% sale, got about 69 hours worth of playtime and probably more if you really enjoy doing all the side content. Got what I paid for, recommended
So I have all three games and most if not all the dlc on disc back during the 360 era. Absolutely adored it then, still adore it here~

By far the best value I've ever gotten out of $6 (frequent sale price) and I haven't even gotten to the 3rd game yet. I'd been meaning to play these games for 15 years and just kept putting it off. I'm glad to say, these games have aged beautifully. The story and world are great sci-fi, decisions actually matter and make differences that carry throughout the trilogy, and the combat is extremely fun and strategically satisfying. The insanity difficulty feels like the right amount of challenge without being frustrating. I can't think of any other shooter that's had as in depth of RPG mechanics as Mass Effect.

I don't normally care about the story so much but this game was the first time I really got engaged in the story to the point I couldn't multitask w/ TV or skip cutscenes. It's very cool, I hope they make a show out of it or something.

I love that with this Legendary Edition it's really easy to continue with the same character, importing all your decisions and stuff across the series so that they compound, super cool idea.

I'll say as a warning that I absolutely loved ME1 but I lost steam during ME2. The gameplay is significantly worse and there are a lot of sidequests that are just a slog. I do want to pick it up again so I can make it to ME3 though because I've heard it gets better again.

☑ https://vojtastruhar.github.io/steam-review-template

My brother in Christ, this is the greatest space opera ever written. 

Play it. 

Do you really need to read a review to convince you?

Fine. 

The Steam Version of this Trilogy is the 6th way that I own Mass Effect, and currently has the least number of hours accumulated in it. The only reason it doesn't have more right now is because I'm binging Pokémon Champions like a Boy Scout binges cases of mountain dew. Why do I own 6 copies of Mass Effect? Because it's the greatest space opera ever written. 

The Good: 

The Characters -  Every RPG needs a solid cast of characters, and Bioware outdoes every other sci-fi in Mass Effect's orbit with their cast and casting. Every squadmate introduced across this series has an astounding amount of depth to be explored by you, the great Commander Shepard, as you go blasting across the Milky Way to save the galaxy. Every Mass Effect fan will try to tell you why their niche squadmate is actually the best, and will have the ability to back it up with a multi-page master's thesis, which is a testament to Bioware's superb ability to write characters. The depth of each character also means that there's some worthwhile story that comes from investing time in interacting with them. **This means** that you shouldn't write off Ashley for being a xenophobe, Jack for being an ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, or Jacob for being... Jacob. Give them a chance. 

The Gameplay - This is a third person shooter cover shooter, but boy-howdy are there a lot of ways to play. Every class is unique in its feel. There's a dozen different ways to tackle your enemies as Shepard as you mix up your powers, loud-outs, and squadmates. Make sure you're running a different class every time you play and I promise you'll never get bored. 

The Story - It has its faults, but overall this thing is an epic for the ages. This is a choose-your-own adventure narrative layered over a geo-political military thriller about saving the galaxy from an existential threat. How you choose to play Commander Shepard has a direct outcome on how events play out. Now, you could choose to go goody two-shoes Shepard the whole route for the best ending like the majority of people who play through this series. Or, hear me out. You could wreck some ♥♥♥♥. Make some renegade choices. Ruin the galaxy a little. When Shepard makes a deliberately bad choice, the galaxy is permanently reshaped and it's thrilling to see how that plays out. This trilogy is like inFamous on steroids. Take advantage. If you really feel that bad about it, you can always have your paragon run in the background to run back to. 

The Music - The soundtrack of this game is SO GOOD. It's not something to describe, it's just something to experience. No other sci-fi game can compare (looking at you, Outer Wilds). Hop in and just lose yourself for a bit. 

The World Building - Something that Mass Effect has always had on lock when compared to other sci-fi games is its world building. Mass Effect's world building is easily accessible and always alive. When you explore the worlds of Mass Effect, you can see the cultures of the Milky Way pop to life. Their architecture, their clothing, their mannerisms and the way they interact with each other. These are present throughout the series, and Bioware builds upon them game after game. ME3 really reaches a pinnacle like no other, but it is present from when you first step foot in ME1.

The Dialogue - With great characters comes great dialogue, but the dialogue in this series is truly in a league of its own. Each character has witty dialogue, but it's not some c/p garbage where everyone feels the same. Every character has their own mannerisms that convey their personalities, and later titles allow you to witness small interactions between squadmates and NPCs that really bring the whole world to life.

Reshaping Companions - Because this game is a choose-your-own-adventure at its core where Bioware stresses that your choices matter, one of the amazing side effects of Mass Effect is your ability, as the player, to reshape the Squadmates that you adventure with. Some characters will be more susceptible than others to your influence, but all of them will be influenced by your actions, both direct and indirect. One of the prime examples of this is how you can, to quote the Youtuber Big Dan Gaming, "♥♥♥♥♥-whip Kaiden Alenko into being a xenophobe" in ME1. 

The Bad: 

The Mako – This is a vehicle from ME1. Some people love it. I don’t. 

Limited Perspective & Dialogue Choices - While Mass Effect is a game about choices, one of the persistent frustrations I have with the series (especially in ME2) is how artificial some of the perspective and dialogue limitations on Shepard feel from a player perspective. There are characters Shepard comes across where it feels as though you, the player, should be allowed to be incredibly aggressive towards these characters because of context. However, the option doesn't exist. These restrictions are for the flow of the narrative that Bioware has constructed, and while that makes sense, it still creates some frustration. [100% ME1's Side Quests as a Sole Survivor, then play ME2. You'll see what I mean].

ME1's Lack of Map Markers - ME2 & ME3 introduce map markers on the Galaxy Map to keep track of quests and missions, but ME1 doesn't get any of that, which makes keeping track of where everything is a monumental nightmare. You CAN fix this with mods on PC but the console versions get no such reprieve. 

ME2's Insanity Difficulty - This isn't a complaint about how hard it is. ME2's Insanity is just the most obnoxious Insanity out of the Trilogy. This is because it gives a variety of Shield Modifiers to every enemy in the game, from the grunts all the way to the bosses. This means that your AoE abilities on classes like Adept and Sentinel are much harder to use, because in ME2 you must break through all shields and armor for certain abilities to affect opponents. It's not impossible to use them, but they're a lot more niche in their applications. 

ME3's Conclusion Controversy - There was some controversy when ME3 was released regarding its ending. The controversy has since died down. Whether you research it is up to you, but you deserve to know it existed and not everyone is happy with the way ME3 ends. 

Stumbling Into Relationships - There are a few dialogue options that activate romances which aren't necessarily clear to the player in ME1 and ME2. These are more notorious for BroShep than FemShep. If you want to avoid an awkward interaction down the line, you may want to save before interacting with Ashley, Liara, or Jack. 

Not Clapping for Mordin!? - In ME2, you pick up a companion called Mordin Solus. Through interacting with him, you'll find out that Mordin used to perform musical theater, and he'll sing a bit about being a scientist. It's a charming couple of lines about his work and passion, and at the end, you, as Command Shepard, STAND THERE AWKWARDLY LIKE A ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ AND DON'T SAY ANYTHING! The least you could do is CLAP FOR THE MAN. Is there a mod that fixes this? I'M GLAD YOU ASKED, YES THERE IS. You should download it. 

If you're still reading this, props to you. Mass Effect is a stellar experience. It hits homeruns on the aspects that matter, and the parts that frustrate aren't dealbreakers. Combine this with a modding community that still works at the game to this day, and you've got a product that's continuing to evolve well past when its original developers have called it quits. This is the greatest space opera ever written. You deserve to experience it for yourself. Buy the damn game and play it. 

And don't you dare be mean to Conrad Verner.
All 3 games play great, the remastered graphics are clean, and the story is one of the RPG genre's best. Where is Mass Effect 5?
Excellent game. Great before, much better now! Cheers guys.

Played ME many of moons ago, and to get back to the game with the updated graphics and with all the DLCs so much more content missed whne did the original play though befor the remaster.
and enjoyed the game even more than back in the day.
I'll highley recommend it,
Just finished this game and i have to say the story is a 10/10 it is definetly worth buying even after 5 years of release and even after 19 years when og mass effect first released
A legendary( pun absolutely intended) classic... if you love space, deeply personal storytelling with intergalactic existential threat-levels of stakes... this is the game for you! Nuff said!
из плюсов графика. остальное помои. управление за каким то xyем переделали, звук переделали в худшую сторону

Mass Effect 1- A bit janky but it was very fun. Space felt vast and empty in a good way. So many planets of nothing made the few habitable ones worth so much. The story was enjoyable if not very railroads from what I was expecting on Mass Effects reputation. The characters made the game shine, I wish there were more romance options during the game. The freedom was also a massive plus, its on the player to either explore all they can, connect to the galaxy or just rush the story missions. 

Mass Effect 2- Best one easily, from character choices, armor customizations and so many ways the final mission can play out. It was amazing, more romance options made my day and while the Dlcs are kinda odd, especially the Alpha relay, I thoroughly enjoyed this title. I missed the planet exploration and was looking forward to seeing what they changed, I learned to enjoy the minigame of resource gathering and system searching. The story has changed to shades of gray in comparison of the black and white telling's of 1 and I loved it. From the jump there is ethical dilemmas from new crew and old. Overall the tone was much darker and mature, I had several missions that made me take a deep breath and take a needed break. The ending was amazing and made me so excited for the final game. 

Mass Effect 3- Oof. From the start, it didn't really feel like a continuation of the progress of the last 2 games. The jump from 1 to 2 was felt and you can see the changes and directional flow the devs were making. This game however felt a lot more like they were trying to copy trends and others success. I spent the first several hours of the game pinpointing all the changes that made me think of other similar titles, it felt less like an original Mass Effect experience and more like an attempt to hide the rushing they had to do. The game play was fun, the combat was somehow jankier than the first game though. The amount of times I got annoyed at the cornering movement when trying to go anywhere was too many to count. The game has a lot of freedom but was so weird when it came to figuring out where you needed to go, if its a straight line it will give you a massive beacon to follow, the second its kinda hard to figure out, no help. They also had no warnings on any of the Dlc that lock you into hours of unescapable content some of which had major spoilers to events that hadn't happened yet in the story. The Ending is all anyone can talk about and that's for a good reason. I won't spoil anything with my thoughts on it. Unsatisfactory. Rushed. Not what my Shepard would do. I was surprised by the endings, I had played my Shepard in a pretty obvious way from the beginning of 1, a path I know so many others had followed and to not of a single ending that fits it was.... sad. I finish this trilogy with a mild amount of bad taste in the mouth. A sadness that all the things my Shepard and I wanted to do couldn't be done, an ending I wouldn't have chosen if I was allowed. 

Mass Effect Trilogy- Solid 8.5 out of 10

 Keelah se'lai

My favorite scifi-universe in its definitive form, hard not to love. The fact that they managed to "fix" the combat of the first game is enough to warrant this package. The games just keep getting better one after another, but sadly the paragon/renegade-system of the third game was not applied to the previous two games, so they sort of "force" you to pick a good or a bad playthrough until you get to the last game, where picking a side is not really a thing, so you can actually roleplay and pick your answers according to your own morals and values. But there might be a mod to fix the paragon/renegade-systems of the first two games, but I didn't come across any.

Other than that, what is there to say, except these games are amazing to experience. I've got like five playthroughs of the trilogy, sixth one finishing up soon. Just a fantastic universe and fantastic characters, there's literally something for everyone in these games and boy is the third game a real tear-jerker, never fails to make me cry.

A must-play for any RPG-fan. Cannot recommend this trilogy enough and this is the best version of it to play.
good game. Tali is great. PLEASE MAKE IT RUN ON APPLE PLEASE

Got this game during a sale when a friend recommended it, thought "Eh, cheep enough, even though I've never had any real interest, I'll get it", and boy am I glad I did.
After 2 full playthroughs, I gotta say, I am extremely sad that there are no other games like Mass Effect out there.
The story is amazing, likewise are the characters, the choices, and especially the emotional impact.

The way the choices carry over into the next games, it's simply perfect.
I won't spoil anything, but events hint to the next games, make your choices carefully.
Especially if you want to RP a character that makes the choices from the perspective of Shepard in the moment.

Sure, some game aspects are iffy, meh, or even bad, such as the literal mining fetch side missions in ME1, but the scans in ME2 and ME3 get too much flak, since it doesn't take a lot of time if you do each sector when you progress to a mission in it.
And none of it diminishes the value of the games.

Amazing game, but fair warning none of the endings are good. Also, there are several mods you should consider installing. At least the community patches. Check them out here
 https://www.reddit.com/r/masseffect/comments/10jcfhn/mass_effect_legendary_edition_mods_recommendations/
immersive gameplay and excellent storyline

The Mass Effect series is not perfect, in fact, it's really far from being perfect, there are a few plot holes, poorly written stories and characters (♥♥♥♥ you ,Jacob) and the gameplay of the first game aged like milk stored next to an uninsulated boiler. This begs the question: why do so many of us love this series and consider it one of the best trilogy in the history of games? Because it manages to overcome it's flaws and ends up being a coherent and beautiful story. It doesn't matter of something has 10 issues or 10000, if it manages to resonate with your soul, then it was worth it in the end, and I absolute encourage everyone to play through this masterpiece at least once.

What is there to say about the Mass Effect Trilogy that has not been said?  One of the best space opera's in any medium, interesting storylines following a charismatic, brave and principled protagonist, cover based squad tactics that are effective and not overly complicated, and affecting, emotive cutscenes that help you bond not only with the lead character, but also all the supporting characters as well.

The Legendary edition brings all three of the games under one umbrella with some improvements to the earlier games, and makes one want to just play all three, on after another, like binge watching a great sci-fi series but better, interactive, as it can only be with games.
